name: The Lighthouse Way: Corme - Ponteceso Loop
name_original: Corme – sendero por Duna de A Barra y Islas de Balarés
description: Hike the difficult 8.9-mile Corme trail through Duna de A Barra and Islas de Balarés, featuring coastal paths, dunes, and viewpoints.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte traverse a dramatic coastline in the northwest of Galicia, Spain, where imposing cliffs meet the Atlantic Ocean. The region is characterized by unspoiled beaches, extensive sand dunes, and estuaries, providing diverse scenery for hikers. Wetlands are crucial habitats for migratory birds, adding to the natural appeal of the landscape. Best easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte The most popular easy hiking route is Playa de Area Maior – View of Seaia Beach loop from Malpica, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes by beaches. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dunas de Corrubedo – Beach dune loop from Illa dos Cagallóns, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail explores the unique dune landscape and coastal areas. Local hikers also love the Artificial Turf in As Grelas loop from Illa dos Cagallóns, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking in Costa Da Morte Norte is defined by rugged coastal paths, pristine beaches, and diverse dune systems. The network offers options for various ability levels, including many easy routes suitable for all. The routes in Costa Da Morte Norte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 340 reviews. More than 2,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Costa Da Morte Norte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Costa Da Morte Norte offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 80 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, the region features more than 200 hiking routes for various skill levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: The easy trails in Costa Da Morte Norte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 340 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic coastal views, unspoiled beaches, and diverse natural features found along these routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy easy hikes in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: The best time to enjoy easy hikes in Costa Da Morte Norte is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, but trails may be busier.

Q: Are there easy circular routes in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Costa Da Morte Norte are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20157830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Area Maior – View of Seaia Beach loop from Malpica&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dunas de Corrubedo – Beach dune loop from Illa dos Cagallóns&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy trails in Costa Da Morte Norte suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. The easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte are generally suitable for families, offering manageable distances and gentle elevation changes. Routes that explore beaches, dunes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3785255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Branco Dune&lt;/a&gt;, or coastal paths are often great choices for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Many easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte are dog-friendly, especially those in natural areas and along the coast. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in protected areas or near wildlife habitats, and to check local regulations for specific beaches or natural parks.

Q: Are there easy trails with waterfalls in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Yes, Costa Da Morte Norte is home to the unique Ézaro Waterfall, the only one in Europe that flows directly into the Atlantic Ocean. While the main guide focuses on easy hikes, there are accessible paths and viewpoints around the waterfall, offering an impressive natural sight.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Easy hikes in Costa Da Morte Norte showcase a diverse range of natural features. You'll encounter dramatic coastal cliffs, pristine sandy beaches, extensive sand dunes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3785255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Branco Dune&lt;/a&gt;, and estuaries that are crucial habitats for migratory birds. The region's wild and untouched natural beauty is a constant companion.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along easy trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Along coastal walks, you might encounter the poignant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;English Cemetery (Cementerio de los Ingleses)&lt;/a&gt;, a monument to shipwreck victims. The iconic lighthouses, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3148302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Vilán Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Nariga Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, also offer significant historical and scenic interest.

Q: Are there easy trails accessible by public transport in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: While some larger towns in Costa Da Morte Norte have public transport connections, many of the more secluded easy hiking trails may require private transport. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific trailheads or consider carpooling if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hikes in Costa Da Morte Norte?
A: Many popular easy trailheads and coastal access points in Costa Da Morte Norte offer designated parking areas. For example, routes starting near villages or beaches often have available parking. It's always a good idea to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking at the trailhead.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, many easy hiking trails in Costa Da Morte Norte pass through or near charming fishing villages like Muros, Carnota, and Lires. These villages offer opportunities to find local cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and experience local life after your hike.
tips: Between the Dunes and next to Monte Branco there is a path along which a hiking route runs from Ponteceso to A Barra beach.
View of Tiñosa Island from Barra beach, dune peninsula in the Anllons river month
View of Isla Estrella and beach off the coast of Corme and Punta de Furna

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking near the starting points in Corme, or closer to the Duna de A Barra and Balarés beach areas. Specific parking spots might be available near the access points to the beaches or the Monte Branco dune. Public transport options to Corme, A Barra, or Balarés might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach the trailheads.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While parts of the broader PR-G 148 route are considered low difficulty, the 14.4 km segment that includes the Duna de A Barra and Islas de Balarés is rated as difficult. It requires good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes due to varied terrain and significant elevation changes. It's not ideal for beginners looking for an easy stroll.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter expansive sandy beaches like A Barra and Balarés, impressive dune systems including the vast Duna de Monte Branco with its fine, white sand, and easily navigable cliff areas. There are also ascents to viewpoints and sections along the Anllóns river estuary.

faqs.question: What unique natural features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in unique features. You'll experience the Monte Branco Dune (Duna de Monte Branco), one of Europe's highest rampart dunes. Keep an eye out for the enigmatic Pedra da Serpe (Serpent Stone), an ancient rock carving. The Anllóns river estuary is an important ornithological wetland, offering opportunities for birdwatching, especially around the Isla de los Cagallóns. You'll also get stunning coastal views, showcasing the 'bravura del mar' (roughness of the sea) of the Costa da Morte.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during spring or autumn when the weather is milder and more stable, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially rougher seas and winds of winter. These seasons also offer good conditions for birdwatching in the estuary and enjoying the coastal flora.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many coastal trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, specific regulations may apply in sensitive natural areas like the Duna de Monte Branco and the Anllóns river estuary, which is a bird sanctuary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in these areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in this area, especially within Costa da Morte?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the natural areas within the Costa da Morte region. However, as it passes through protected natural spaces like the Duna de Monte Branco and the Anllóns river estuary (an ornithological wetland), hikers are expected to follow responsible outdoor ethics, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and packing out all trash.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route forms part of the larger PR-G 148 "Ruta do mar de Corme e ribeira do Anllóns" between Corme and Ponteceso. It also intersects with sections of other trails such as Mtb O Camiño dos Faros: Etapa 2 (Niñóns-Ponteceso) and O Camiño dos Faros: Etapa 2 Niñóns-Ponteceso.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to exquisite panoramic views, especially from elevated points like Monte da Facha and Alto de as Travesas. These offer vistas of Ponteceso, Cabana, and the Ría de Corme y Laxe, including distant sights of Laxe's lighthouse and Corme's Punta Roncudo. You'll also get a View of Isla Tiñosa and a View of Estrela Island.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The towns of Corme and Ponteceso, located at either end or near the route, offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. Balarés also has some facilities, especially during peak season, being a popular beach area. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer sections of the trail.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, sturdy hiking shoes are essential. Layers of clothing are recommended due to coastal weather changes. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a windbreaker or rain jacket. Binoculars would be great for birdwatching in the estuary and enjoying distant views.

waypoints.name: Monte Branco Dune (Duna de Monte Branco)
